laws.title: Public Law 30-224 | Vacancies and Acting Officer Reform Act
laws.status_basis: The act rewrites the acting-officer framework, including exclusivity, holdover, notification, and effective-date rules, and nothing in the source body self-repeals the enactment.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the \"Vacancies and Acting Officer Reform Act\""; "The purpose of this legislation is to reform the process for appointing acting officers"
provisions.notes: Introductory matter only.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals most of 5 U.S.C. subchapter III and declares conflicting prior law void.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal 5 U.S. Code subchapter III in its entirety except for sections 3341-3344, and treat conflicting prior law as repealed unless expressly excepted.
provisions.evidence: "5 U.S. Code Subchapter III shall be repealed in its entirety, except for § 3341-3344"
provisions.notes: This is an express textual repeal, not a note.

provisions.text_summary: Sets the acting-officer order and first-assistant rule.
provisions.exact_change: Rewrite the acting-officer rule to make the first assistant, and then the President's designated officer or employee, serve temporarily subject to the Act's limits.
provisions.evidence: "the first assistant to the office of such officer shall perform the functions and duties of the office temporarily in an acting capacity"
provisions.notes: Core operative amendment.

provisions.text_summary: Sets time limits, exclusivity, holdover positions, exclusions, notification rules, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "Sections 4 and 5 of this Act are the exclusive means"; "This Act shall become effective upon enactment"
provisions.notes: These provisions are uncodified governance rules.

current_implementation.detail: The current branch already contains the acting-officer integration in 5 U.S.C. 3345 and the related note history; origin/main does not reflect the same integrated state.

recommended_actions: Keep the integrated acting-officer text in place.
Retain only concise note material for the timing language.
Remove any dump-style note baggage if it still exists.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act rewrites the acting-officer framework, including exclusivity, holdover, notification, and effective-date rules, and nothing in the source body self-repeals the enactment.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep the integrated acting-officer text in place. | Retain only concise note material for the timing language. | Remove any dump-style note baggage if it still exists.

laws.exact_change: Repeal 5 U.S. Code subchapter III in its entirety except for sections 3341-3344, and treat conflicting prior law as repealed unless expressly excepted. | Rewrite the acting-officer rule to make the first assistant, and then the President's designated officer or employee, serve temporarily subject to the Act's limits.

provisions.text_summary: Defines proscribed felonies, principal head, and cooperative agreement.

laws.title: Public Law 31-226 | Commerce and Labor Administrative Reform Act (CLARA)
laws.status_basis: The source body contains a broad Commerce and Labor reorganization, explicit repealments of prior public laws, and a direct repeal of 5 U.S.C. 101, with no self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, findings, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This bill shall be referred to by its short title"; "Congress makes and declares"
provisions.notes: Introductory material only.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals a package of prior public laws.
provisions.target: Public Law 4-29; Public Law 12-80; Public Law 15-92; Public Law 17-126; Public Law 19-152; Public Law 25-183
provisions.exact_change: Repeal the listed prior public laws in their entirety.
provisions.evidence: "Public Law 4-29 shall be repealed, in its entirety"; "Public Law 25-183 shall be repealed, in its entirety"
provisions.notes: These are historical repeal records, not a positive-law Code rewrite.

provisions.text_summary: Creates the Department of Commerce and Labor and transfers Commerce/Labor authorities and related title structure.
provisions.exact_change: Establish the Department of Commerce and Labor, allocate the Commerce and Labor departments and listed agencies into it, and apply the stated title-level restructuring across the cited titles.
provisions.evidence: "Under the Executive Branch there shall be a department known as the Department of Commerce and Labor"; "Title 15"; "Title 29"; "Title 35"; "Title 47"; "Title 51"; "Title 19"
provisions.notes: Broad structural rewrite with multiple determinable code targets.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 5 U.S.C. 101.
provisions.evidence: "5 U.S. Code § 101 shall be repealed in its entirety"
provisions.notes: This is a direct textual repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Sets government labor-management policy and enforcement rules.
provisions.evidence: "The government finds and declares that an effective collective"; "The provisions of this section shall become effective upon the"
provisions.notes: Programmatic policy language, not a clean positive-law section rewrite.

current_implementation.detail: The current branch already contains the integrated 5 U.S.C. 101 Commerce and Labor change and the related title-level restructuring; origin/main does not need a duplicate change.

recommended_actions: Keep the integrated text in place.
Do not duplicate the Commerce and Labor restructuring.
Retain only concise historical note material for the repealed prior laws.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source body contains a broad Commerce and Labor reorganization, explicit repealments of prior public laws, and a direct repeal of 5 U.S.C. 101, with no self-repeal or sunset.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep the integrated text in place. | Do not duplicate the Commerce and Labor restructuring. | Retain only concise historical note material for the repealed prior laws.

laws.title: The Federal Pay Standardization Act
laws.status_basis: The source text is complete and reviewable, but the archive classifies this law as a recorded historical enactment. It is the upstream pay-table anchor for the later pay-law overlap.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, definitions, and findings.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Federal Pay Standardization Act"; SEC 2 defines terms; SEC 3 states Congress finds that...

provisions.text_summary: Establishes an Office of Personnel Management framework and director structure.
provisions.evidence: There is established as an independent establishment in the executive branch an Office of Personnel Management.
provisions.notes: No determinable numbered Code host is identified in the source text.

provisions.text_summary: Replaces the 5 U.S.C. 5332(a) General Schedule table with a single-rate GS-1 through GS-15 table.
provisions.exact_change: Strike the existing General Schedule table in subsection (a) and insert the enacted grade/pay table for GS-1 through GS-15.
provisions.evidence: Section 5(a) says 5 U.S.C. 5332(a) "is amended by striking the General Schedule and inserting the following".

provisions.text_summary: Implementation and regulations for the new pay system.
provisions.evidence: The Director of the Office of Personnel Management shall take such actions as may be necessary to implement the amendments made by this Act.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective-date clause.
provisions.evidence: This Act shall become effective immediately following a majority approval by both the Senate and House of Representatives along with the signature of the President of the United States.

current_implementation.detail: The current branch already contains the integrated 5 U.S.C. 5332 text; origin/main does not. Later law chronology supersedes this enactment, but the branch state itself is already integrated.

recommended_actions: No additional change.
Keep the already incorporated General Schedule text in place.
Retain the enactment only as historical chronology.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source text is complete and reviewable, but the archive classifies this law as a recorded historical enactment. It is the upstream pay-table anchor for the later pay-law overlap.
laws.final_recommended_action: No additional change. | Keep the already incorporated General Schedule text in place. | Retain the enactment only as historical chronology.
